info.name: hobby-track API
info.description:
End-to-end test collection for the hobby-track backend.

## Setup

1. Start the API: `pnpm --filter @hobby-track/api dev`
2. (Optional) Reset DB to clean state: `pnpm --filter @hobby-track/api db:reset-dev`
3. In Postman, ensure cookies are managed automatically (Settings → General → Automatically follow redirects: ON; cookie jar is on by default).
4. Run requests in order — `Login` sets a cookie that all subsequent requests reuse.

## Variables

- `baseUrl` — defaults to `http://localhost:3000`
- `password` — defaults to `changeme-dev-only` (change to match your `APP_PASSWORD`)
- `itemId`, `bookId`, `wishlistBookId` — captured automatically by tests in earlier requests

## Folders

- **0. Auth** — login / me / logout
- **1. Items — Create & Read** — basic CRUD reads
- **2. Items — List Filters** — `?type`, `?status`, `?search`, `?sort`, pagination
- **3. Items — Update & Side Effects** — verifies `lastTouchedAt`, `startedAt`, `completedAt` auto-set
- **4. Active Limit (book limit=2)** — 409 + `?force=true` override on POST and PATCH
- **5. Settings** — GET / PATCH / validation
- **6. Delete** — 204 + 404 on second delete
- **7. Auth Enforcement** — clear cookies in Postman first, then run

Most requests have a `Tests` tab that asserts status code and (where useful) body shape — run with the Collection Runner to see pass/fail across the suite.

exec: pm.test('startedAt set when created as active', () => pm.expect(body.startedAt).to.not.be.null);
pm.test('all returned items are games', () => body.items.forEach(i => pm.expect(i.type).to.eql('game')));
for (let i = 1; i < ps.length; i++) pm.expect(ps[i]).to.be.at.most(ps[i-1]);

item.name: Create active book C with ?force=true (saves bookId)
PATCH wishlist book D to active (409)
PATCH wishlist book D to active with ?force=true (200)
7. Auth Enforcement (clear cookies first)
item.description: Before running this folder, clear cookies in Postman (Cookies icon next to Send → manage cookies → delete the localhost auth_token entry).

item.name: 8. Search (External APIs)
item.description: Phase 3: proxies to RAWG (games), Jikan (anime), Google Books (books). Auth required. Each returns { results: SearchResult[], cached: boolean }. RAWG needs RAWG_API_KEY in .env; Jikan/Google Books work without keys (Google supports an optional key for higher quotas). Cache TTL is 1h per query, so the second hit of the same q+limit returns cached:true.

exec: // Google Books occasionally returns 503 — accept either 200 (success) or 502 (upstream down).

item.name: Search games (RAWG) — 503 if RAWG_API_KEY unset, 200 if set
9. Cover Upload
item.description: Phase 4: POST /api/items/:id/cover — multipart/form-data, single file field (any name). sharp resizes to ≤1200px wide and converts to webp. Old local cover is deleted on re-upload. GET /uploads/:filename serves the result. Run folder 1 first so itemId is populated.

formdata.description: Pick any jpeg/png/webp image from your machine
Pick any jpeg/png/webp image from your machine
